analysis_description: Round-3 tape mids for VEV_4000 and VEV_4500 vs VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT: Black–Scholes implied vol (no dividends, zero rate) and call delta from numerical IV inversion. TTE per calendar day index: historical file day k uses TTE = 7 − k days (7-day voucher clock; aligns with round3description example mapping day index to TTE). Spread stats from bid_price_1 / ask_price_1.
used_to_decide: VEV_4000/4500 show persistently wide top spreads (~21 and ~16 ticks) vs penny edge, supporting deep passive quotes. Mean BS call delta from mids ~0.994 on both strikes (deep ITM), so extract hedge skew is small but nonzero. Chose to anchor passive on 4000/4500 and use extract as delta ballast.

analysis_description: Small parameter grid on backtest: (v0) WIDE_SPREAD_MIN=18 vs (v1) 16; deep offset 7/5 vs symmetric 6; passive size 2 vs 3. Counted VEV_4500 rows in trades_round_3_day_*.csv: essentially no market prints for 4500 on days 0 and 2, one trade on day 1 — explains zero PnL contribution under worse-fill matching despite wide quotes.
used_to_decide: Lower spread threshold to 16 and size 3 improved merged Round-3 PnL (27938 vs 27014). VEV_4500 remains non-actionable in this historical tape for maker-style fills; thesis stays on wide-book passive on 4000 with 4500 quoted for completeness.

analysis_description: Verified parameter mini-grid centered on best parent v1; maintained IV/Greek framework from iteration 0 (BS implied vol + delta on VEV_4000/4500 from tape mids, TTE mapping from round3description). Grid dimensions: VEV4000 deep offset {5,6,7}, passive size {3,4}, extract passive size {4,6}, hydro passive size {1,2}, with spread gate fixed at 16 and VEV4500 offset fixed at 6 due to sparse 4500 trade prints.
used_to_decide: Best verified config improved total PnL from 27,938 to 28,867 by making VEV_4000 quotes slightly less deep (offset 5). This captures more fills while still monetizing very wide spread regimes; kept strategy concept unchanged.

analysis_description: Extended analyze_tapes.py with Black–Scholes call vega (S*phi(d1)*sqrt(T)) at implied IV from mids, same TTE map (day k → 7−k days). tape_vev4000_4500_stats.json now includes vega_mean per strike/day (e.g. ~10–13 at Round-3 TTEs). Backtest mini-grid over DELTA_SKEW×VEGA_SKEW on extract hedge; best merged PnL 29,147 vs v4 28,867.
used_to_decide: Tape shows both strikes still carry material vega despite delta≈0.994; added vega-weighted term to extract skew. Lower delta skew 0.35→0.3 improved day-2 extract PnL vs v4 (+292 on day 2 in backtest), raising three-day total.

analysis_description: analyze_tapes.py: BS call gamma φ(d1)/(S·σ·√T) at implied IV from mids; tape_vev4000_4500_stats.json now includes gamma_mean (order ~1e-4 to 1e-5 in these days). sweep_iter6_tier_gamma.json: backtested second passive tier (WIDE_SPREAD_DEEP, OFF_DEEP2, PASSIVE_SIZE2) and GAMMA_SKEW on extract; GAMMA_SKEW>0 reduced total PnL vs v5; second tier with GAMMA_SKEW=0 matched v5 (29,147).
used_to_decide: Kept v5 extract hedge; document gamma in tape. Added a spread-conditioned second deep quote layer (wide-book passivity) with limit-safe sizing; do not nudge extract from gamma. Performance ties best total with simpler v5; v6 is the same PnL with the extra structural option for very wide books.

analysis_description: Extended tape greek thread: analyze_tapes.py now also computes BS call theta (annual, r=0) and per-day IV vertical skew IV(4000)-IV(4500) from implied vols at shared timestamps; skew is strongly positive and rises day0->day2 (~0.327 -> ~0.384). Focused v5 parameter sweep (sweep_iter7_focused.json) over spread gate, deep offsets, and passive size found best around shallower deep quotes.
used_to_decide: Shallower passive placement (4000:+4, 4500:+5) materially improved fills and total PnL while preserving wide-book passive behavior; v7 improves 29,147 -> 30,076.

analysis_description: VEV Greek thread unchanged (BS IV/delta/vega/gamma/theta + IV(4000)-IV(4500) skew in tape_vev4000_4500_stats.json). Added HYDROGEL_PACK order-book spread rollups in analyze_tapes.py: per day k, mean and median of ask1-bid1 across all hydro ticks; mean ~15.70-15.74 and median 16 on days 0-2, supporting a hydro spread gate at or just below 16. Backtest grid on v7 template: HYDRO_WIDE_MIN in {14,15,16,17} by HYDRO_PASSIVE_SIZE in {1,2,3} (sweep_iter8_hydro.json).
used_to_decide: Median hydro spread is 16, so a gate of 16 leaves roughly half the ticks ineligible; lowering HYDRO_WIDE_MIN to 15 and raising passive size 2->3 (still <<200 limit) raises hydro PnL in the backtest; merged three-day total 30,076 -> 36,500. Kept 15 over 14 to align with the VEV wide-spread threshold.

analysis_description: REQUIRED duplicate-check against parent index 13 branch (origin/r3v_realized_vol_regime_13): parent already explored RV-IV regime widths, smile-theo mispricing takes across strikes, and optional theta/gamma scaling with multiple sweeps to v29. Material overlap risk with my earlier v9 was high, so I stopped and switched to a nearby but distinct family13 variant. New variant keeps RV-IV minimal but changes assumptions: calm aggressive fills require L1 imbalance confirmation and stressed regime turns HYDROGEL_PACK off entirely (risk-off), while vouchers remain passive/wider. Optimized under worse-fill with a small grid on stress cut, imbalance gates, and calm edge thresholds.
used_to_decide: Best run v10c from sweep_iter10_regime_imbalance.json: total 56,804 with parameters cut=-0.50, imbalance gates extract/hydro 0.20/0.25, calm edges 4/7. This satisfies distinctness from parent (different execution assumptions and instrument risk-off policy) while preserving family13 RV-IV regime framing. Still below ~800k target, so further iterations should improve signal quality and strike coverage.
